papers.title: Genetic basis controlling rice plant architecture and its modification for breeding
papers.authors: Wakana Tanaka, Takaki Yamauchi, Katsutoshi Tsuda

papers.title: Potential of rice tillering for sustainable food production

edges.evidence: Knockdown and knockout mutants of AtPIN1 orthologues in rice, OsPIN1a and OsPIN1b, increase tiller numbers
Knockdown and knockout mutants of AtPIN1 orthologues in rice, OsPIN1a and OsPIN1b, increase tiller numbers
auxin up-regulates D17/HTD1 and D10 via AUXIN RESISTANT1–TRANSPORT INHIBITOR RESPONSE1 (AXR1–TIR1)
auxin up-regulates D17/HTD1 and D10
D10 encodes CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8 (CCD8) involved in SL biosynthesis
D17/HIGH-TILLERING DWARF1 (HTD1) acts as enzyme in SL biosynthesis
DWARF27 acts as enzyme in SL biosynthesis
Upon entering axillary buds, SLs are perceived by the D14 protein
Reception of SLs by D14 triggers formation of protein complex of D14, D3, and D53 resulting in ubiquitination and degradation of D53
D3 (F-box protein) in SCF complex ubiquitinates D53 via 26S proteasome
D53 physically interacts with IDEAL PLANT ARCHITECTURE1 (IPA1)/WFP to inhibit its transcriptional activity
IPA1 negatively regulates bud elongation by promoting the expression of OsTB1
OsTB1 positively regulate the expression of DWARF14 (D14)
OsTB1 negatively regulates axillary bud elongation during tiller formation
Upon up-regulation of OsTB1/FC1, its protein enhances expression of GRASSY TILLER1 (GT1) by directly binding
OsGT1 is likely to regulate genes such as OsNCED1 that encode abscisic acid biosynthesis enzymes
NCED1 encodes abscisic acid biosynthesis enzymes
ABA involved in axillary bud dormancy and suppression of outgrowth
BR is known to stabilize the OsBZR1 protein
OsBZR1 recruits D53 to repress the expression of OsTB1 - but in presence of BR stabilizing OsTB1 expression
SL treatment triggers the degradation of OsBZR1
auxin down-regulates the expression of pea IPT family genes through AXR–TIR-dependent auxin signaling pathway
IPT catalyzes rate-limiting step of cytokinin biosynthesis
CK is considered to promote bud elongation; cytokinins are transported acropetally in xylem, and enter axillary buds and promote outgrowth
CK is likely to be involved in tiller formation partly via negative regulation of OsTB1
OsCKX2 encodes cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase that degrades cytokinin
multiple members of cytokinin oxidase genes including OsCKX4 repress tiller outgrowth through CK degradation
OsCKX9 expression is triggered by SL and degrades cytokinin
OsCKX11 degrades cytokinin
OsCKX9 functions as a primary strigolactone-responsive gene
GA-deficient and GA-signaling mutants produce more tillers than wild type
both proteins [SLR1 and MOC1] are degraded upon GA treatments
SLR1 interacts with MOC1 to prevent its degradation; the protein level of SLR1 correlates with that of MOC1
NGR5 is destructed upon GA treatments through the proteasome pathway; GA receptor GID1 targets NGR5 for proteolysis
SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 and prevents NGR5 from GA-mediated proteolysis
MOC1 plays an essential role in the initiation of axillary bud formation
TAD1 recruits MOC1 to the APC/C and then degrades MOC1 in a cell cycle-dependent manner
TE mediates the degradation of MONOCULM 1 via APC/C complex
LAX1 and LAX2 proteins act cooperatively in the premeristem zone to promote axillary meristem formation
LAX2 required for maintenance and formation of axillary meristems
TAB1 promotes stem cell fate in the premeristem zone during axillary meristem formation
FON2 restricts the stem cell population by moderately repressing TAB1 expression
OsWOX4 starts to be expressed in the established axillary meristem and maintains stem cells
NGR5 promotes tillering by directly repressing tillering repressors such as IPA1
NGR5 directly repressing OsTB1 via H3K27me3 modification via PRC2 recruitment
Nitrogen supply promotes tillering via NGR5
OsTCP19 directly represses the expression of DWARF AND LOW TILLERING (DLT)
DLT is an important BR-signaling gene to promote tiller outgrowth
OsTCP19 expression is induced at low nitrogen levels but is repressed at higher concentrations
Auxin-induced apical dominance requires the activity of BRC1 [OsTB1 ortholog]
Nitrogen fertilizer causes the downregulation of OsTB1 to promote tiller formation
MOC2 encodes a fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase1 responsible for the sucrose biosynthesis pathway
sugars are key regulators of axillary bud elongation; sucrose restricts growth of axillary buds when depleted
sucrose partially repressed the expression of PsBRC1 [OsTB1 ortholog]
japonica allele OsTB23bp+ reduces the expression of D14
OsTB2 protein directly interacts with OsTB1 and possibly interferes with the positive regulatory effect of OsTB1 on D14
CIRCADIAN CLOCK ASSOCIATED1 (OsCCA1) positively controls OsTB1/FC1 expression
SHI1 interacts with IPA1 to inhibit its function
OsMADS57 protein suppresses D14 expression
OsMADS57 protein can interact with OsTB1 (physical interaction, mutual regulation)
OsMADS57 gene is targeted and regulated negatively by miR444a
physical interaction between MOC1 and MOC3 can up-regulate the expression of FLORAL ORGAN NUMBER1 (FON1)
MOC3/TAB1/SRT1 induces tiller initiation
IPA1 is negatively regulated by miR156 (targeting the OsSPL14 mRNA for cleavage)
miR529 cleaves OsSPL14/IPA1 mRNA alongside miR156
OsPIN1b functions as auxin efflux transporter
overexpression of OsPIN2 increased tiller outgrowth
overexpression and knockout of OsPIN9 result in increased and decreased tillering
OsPIN9 expression is induced by ammonium, a major source of nitrogen

perturbations.evidence: loss-of-function mutant of MOC1 exhibits a monoculm phenotype without tillers
Overexpression of MOC1 leads to the formation of higher-order tillers such as tertiary, quaternary, and quinary
LAX1 required for axillary meristem formation
LAX2 required for axillary meristem formation
lax1 lax2 double mutant completely lacks tillers
rice tab1 produces no tillers, while it normally develops the primary shoot
in the fon2 mutant, the sizes of the SAM and the inflorescence meristem were increased
in the FON2 overexpressor, axillary meristem formation is inhibited
mutants in SL signaling commonly show tillering dwarf phenotypes; d3 is dwarf high-tillering
d14 mutant shows increased tillering and dwarfism
d53 mutant is dominant gain-of-function, SL-insensitive, increased tillers
Loss of function mutants of IPA1 show the high tillering and dwarf phenotype
ipa1-1D gain-of-function dominant allele: accumulation of IPA1 leads to ideal plant architecture with decreased tiller number
fc1 mutant (OsTB1 loss-of-function) causes an increase in the number of tillers; NIL-fc1 produces 143% more panicles than Takanari
overexpressor of OsTB1 showed a significant reduction in tiller outgrowth
Knockdown mutants of OsPIN1a increase tiller numbers
Knockout mutants of OsPIN1b increase tiller numbers
overexpression of OsPIN2 increased tiller outgrowth
overexpression of OsPIN9 result in increased tillering
knockout of OsPIN9 result in decreased tillering
Gn1a/OsCKX2 reduced-function allele increases tiller number and grain number
Down-regulation of OsCKX2 expression through RNAi led to enhanced growth and productivity via increased tiller numbers
multiple cytokinin oxidase genes including OsCKX4 repress tiller outgrowth
OsCKX9 represses tiller outgrowth; knockout gives more tillers
OsCKX11 coordinates source-sink; loss increases tiller number
varieties containing the sd1 allele (GA20ox2 null) produce more tillers
loss-of-function mutant of SLR1 exhibit fewer tillers
loss-of-function mutant of TAD1 shows an increased tiller phenotype
TAD1 overexpressors display a reduced tiller number
te mutant shows increased tiller number via stabilized MOC1
ngr5 loss-of-function mutant failed to induce tiller outgrowth in response to nitrogen
allele with higher NGR5 expression increased tiller number and yield
accessions with 29-bp insertion in OsTCP19 promoter show high tillering response to nitrogen
Loss of MOC2 causes a significant reduction in tiller number
moc3 mutant cannot form normal axillary meristems
fon1 mutant forms them normally but fails to elongate tiller buds
d27 mutant classic rice mutant exhibiting increased tillers and reduced plant height
htd1/d17 mutant shows high-tillering dwarf phenotype
d10 mutant shows more tillers than wild type
GR24 (synthetic SL) treatment suppresses bud outgrowth in wild-type rice
exogenous cytokinin (BA or kinetin) promotes axillary bud outgrowth
